data.title: Wall Street slides as tech, consumer discretionary stocks retreat ahead of Tesla results
data.description:
Wall Street slumped on Wednesday, weighed down by a slide in technology and consumer discretionary stocks and a post-earnings plunge in some high-profile companies.

Meanwhile, a continued bond sell-off on bets that the Federal Reserve would not be as aggressive with rate cuts as previously expected also weighed on equities.

The S&P (SP500) was last -1.25% at 5,778.23 points in afternoon trade, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -1.16% at 42,428.25 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) headed for its worst session since early September, -2.05% at 18,192.22 points.

Of the 11 S&P sectors, nine were in the red. Technology and Consumer Discretionary were -2% each and led the losers.

Technology was slammed as chip stocks fell, led by a -7.4% decrease in Arm (ARM). The British chip designer reportedly canceled a license deal that allows Qualcomm (QCOM) to use its intellectual property.

Qualcomm (QCOM) was -4.8%, and other major names such as Marvell Technology (MRVL) -4%, Nvidia (NVDA) -3.9% and Broadcom (AVGO) -3.6% were down. Semiconductor bellwether Texas Instruments (TXN) was +3.3%, bucking the trend as analysts reacted favorably to its quarterly results.

Consumer Discretionary was weighed down by a -5.4% retreat in McDonald's (MCD).

The world's largest fast-food chain has been linked to an E. coli outbreak by the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention that has sickened 49 people across ten states and killed one person. The Dow 30 component was also the primary drag on the blue-chip index.

All eyes are on Tesla (TSLA), with the electric vehicle giant slated to report earnings after the closing bell. For all the hype generated by Tesla's (TSLA) recent robotaxi reveal, investors will still be looking at the metrics of its core electric vehicle business, including the operating margin line. The Magnificent 7 member has significantly underperformed its peers in terms of stock gains.

In another notable move, Apple (AAPL) was -3.4% after TF International Securities' analyst Ming-Chi Kuo said iPhone 16 orders have been cut by around 10M units for the next few quarters.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields advanced as a bond sell-off stretched into yet another day. Traders have been steadily recalibrating their expectations for aggressive Fed rate cuts following strong economic data recently. Comments from well-known Fed speakers this week have expressed a preference for gradual policy easing.

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.23%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.07%.

According to Goldman Sachs FICC and Equities, the rise in the US10Y is also "part of the 'Trump Trade' as GS research points out a red sweep will see a higher 10yr."

"When do equities notice? Historically, a 2 SD move in (the US10Y), equivalent to around 60 bps today (3yr lookback), over a month is when impingement for equity markets kicks in. Given we've moved 46 bps MTD, this simple rule of thumb argues that a move towards 4.30% in where things would get tricky for stocks. Speed of the move matters and keep an eye on real yields," Goldman Sachs FICC and Equities added.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

Looking at Wednesday's economic calendar, the National Association of Realtors said existing home sales slipped 1% in September to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 3.84M.

There was also data from the Atlanta Fed, which indicated that firms' year-ahead inflation expectations in October remained relatively unchanged at 2.2%.

The Fed's Beige Book on U.S. economic activity arrived, showing little change in activity in nearly all districts.

More on the markets

  • Paul Tudor Jones on inflation hedges: Long gold, bitcoin, commodities
  • Are You Looking For Data To Support Your Views?
  • Latest Memo From Howard Marks: Ruminating On Asset Allocation
  • Rising Dividend Outlook Boosting Stock Prices
  • Soaring $1-handle stocks leaping into IBKR’s most-active list may point to market froth

data.title: Amazon Prime members can now pay less for gas
data.description:
• Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) Prime members can now pay less for fuel at certain gas station thanks to a new offer from the ecommerce giant.
  • Prime members are eligible for a 10 cents per gallon discount at 7,000 BP-owned businesses (BP) including Amoco and AM/PM convenience stores after enrolling in the loyalty program Earnify.
  • Once enrolled, customers can get discounts at the pump by entering their phone number or linked payment method.
  • Amazon’s (AMZN) fuel discount is an effort to compete with rival Walmart (WMT) which offers its Walmart+ members a 10 cent per gallon discount at ExxonMobil (XOM) and Murphy (WMT) gas stations.
  • Next year, the program will be expanded to EV charging stations operated by BP Pulse.

data.title: U.S. economic activity appears tepid since early September: Fed's Beige Book
data.description:
• Overall, economic activity was little changed across the Federal Reserve's 12 districts since early September, the central bank observed in its Beige Book report on Wednesday. That compared with flat or declining economic activity in most districts in the previous Beige Book.
  • Two districts reported "modest growth," and most districts reported declining manufacturing activity.
  • Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: PROG Holdings stock slides after Q3 earnings drop more than expected
data.description:
• PROG Holdings (NYSE:PRG) stock slumped 10% in Wednesday afternoon trading after its Q3 earnings missed the average analyst estimate and its guidance boost underwhelmed.
  • The fintech company increased its full-year guidance for non-GAAP EPS to $3.30-$3.40 (midpoint $3.35 vs. consensus estimate of $3.38) from its prior outlook of $3.25-$3.40. Revenue guidance was revised to $2.44B-$2.46B (midpoint $2.45B vs. $2.44B consensus) from $2.40B-$2.46B.
  • Q3 non-GAAP EPS of $0.77 fell short of the $0.80 consensus and declined from $0.90 in last year's Q3.
  • Revenue of $606.1M climbed from $582.9M, beating the average analyst estimate of $602.4M.
  • "Our focus on enhancing both customer and retailer experiences has helped deliver top-line momentum and grow our balance of share with existing retail partners," said President and CEO Steve Michaels.
  • Earlier, PROG Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $0.77 misses by $0.03, revenue of $606.15M beats by $3.73M

data.title: Disc Medicine draws but at Jefferies on lead asset
data.description:
Jefferies initiated its coverage of hematology-focused biotech Disc Medicine (NASDAQ:IRON) with a buy recommendation on Wednesday, citing the potential of its lead candidate, bitopertin, targeted at a rare blood disorder called erythropoietic porphyrias.

Analyst Roger Song argued that backed by “generally supportive” mid-stage data, Disc Medicine (NASDAQ:IRON) is advancing bitopertin into late-stage development.

A meeting between Massachusetts-based Disc Medicine (IRON) and the FDA is scheduled for Q4 2024 to discuss a proposed Phase 3 trial design for bitopertin, Song pointed out.

Additionally, the analyst noted that the company has two earlier-stage candidates targeting other non-malignant hem disorders: anemia of myelofibrosis, chronic kidney disease, and polycythemia vera.

Jefferies issued a $89 price target on Disc Medicine (IRON), estimating a multi-billion dollar peak adjusted revenue opportunity for the company across erythropoietic porphyrias, myelofibrosis, and chronic kidney disease.

data.title: New MacBook Air with M4 chip will come early next year: report
data.description:
• A new Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) MacBook Air with the tech giant's artificial intelligence-focused M4 chip will come in early 2025, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • Apple and its manufacturing partners will soon start producing 13-inch and 15-inch models of the new laptops, the news outlet added, citing sources. It will have a similar look to the current MacBook Air, with the M4 chip being the big update.
  • The company is also working on a new low-cost version of the iPad, which could be released early next year, Bloomberg added.
  • Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple is also set to unveil several new Mac computers next week, all with the M4 chip, as the company boosts its AI-related offerings. These include new versions of the iMac and MacBook Pro models, as well as a refreshed version of the Mac mini.
  • Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: General Dynamics boosts profit on Gulfstream, defense demand
data.description:
General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) boosted earnings and revenue in the third quarter on solid demand for its Gulfstream business jets and defense unit's combat systems.

Shares of General Dynamics (GD) fell as much as 1.7% on Wednesday.

The aerospace and defense company reported net earnings of $930 million, or $3.35 a share, compared with $836 million, or $3.04 a share, a year earlier. The average estimate among Wall Street analysts was $3.51 a share.

Revenue rose 10% to $11.67 billion, in line with the consensus estimate of $11.7 billion.

At the end of the quarter, total estimated contract value was $137.6 billion, including a backlog of $92.6 billion.

Chief Executive Phebe Novakovic said demand remains strong among its business units.

Its Gulfstream Aerospace division delivered 28 aircraft during the quarter, compared with 27 a year earlier, including 24 large-cabin aircraft. Gulfstream began delivering its G700 ultra long-range business jet after receiving certification in March.

The company's defense segment, which is made up of the combat-systems division that makes tanks and other military vehicles, was awarded major contracts as the Pentagon seeks to replenish stockpiles. The rewards include $885 million for munitions and ordnance and $465 million for two U.S. Army contracts to make 155-millimeter artillery parts.

data.title: Billl Ackman says Pershing not placing bets on U.S. presidential race outcome
data.description:
Bill Ackman, founder and CEO of Pershing Square Capital Management, said the firm is not placing bets tied to the outcome of the U.S. presidential election that ends in early November.

“We have no bets in the portfolio on the basis of who's going to be president,” Ackman said in a CNBC interview on Wednesday. “We don't short stocks anymore, as you know. We own these great American businesses.”

The hedge fund boss appeared on CNBC’s “Squawk Box” expressing his support for former President Donald Trump in his Oval Office run against Vice President Kamala Harris.

The contest appears to be in a dead-heat less than two weeks before November 5. In national polling, Harris had a +2 point lead over Trump as of Wednesday, according to the New York Times. The NYT also said the candidates were essentially tied in five so-called battleground states – Pennsylvania, Michigan, Nevada, Wisconsin and North Carolina.

In the betting markets, Trump held an advantage over Harris in odds of winning, at 61% vs 41% on the PredictIt site.

For investors looking to track the elections through market instruments, here are some politically driven Republican and Democratic exchange-traded funds:

  • God Bless America ETF (NYSEARCA:YALL)
  • American Conservative Values ETF (NYSEARCA:ACVF)
  • Point Bridge America First ETF (BATS:MAGA)
  • Democratic Large Cap Core ETF (NASDAQ:DEMZ)
  • Unusual Whales Subversive Democratic ETF (BATS:NANC)
  • Unusual Whales Subversive Republican ETF (BATS:KRUZ)

Here are some Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (IEF), (SHY) and (SGOV).

And some ETFs tracking the S&P 500 (SP500): (IVV), (SPY), (VOO), (RSP) and (SH).

Readers interested in investing topics tied to the upcoming election can find more information at the upcoming Seeking Alpha Investing Forum: Election 2024;please visit the event website.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Cadence Design bid for Altair makes 'less sense' after recent deals: Baird
data.description:
Recent reports suggest that Altair Engineering (NASDAQ:ALTR) is exploring a sale after receiving takeover interest. However, a deal from Cadence Design Systems (NASDAQ:CDNS) makes "less sense" after some recent acquisitions by Cadence, Baird said.

"[While] we appreciate the storyline of Cadence buying Altair after Altair just acquired Joe Costello’s Metrics (a closing of the Cadence circle, if you will...), we have admittedly thought a Cadence+Altair combination made less sense after Cadence acquired BETA CAE and overlapping capability in FEA and auto/aerospace," analyst Joe Vruwink wrote in a note to clients.

Vruwink, who has an Overweight rating on Cadence Design Systems, said he is not "opposed" to Cadence acquiring Altair based on product merits, as a potential deal could be additive to Cadence in new markets, while also actively investing in an electronics portfolio. However, it's likely that there are other suitsors for Altair, given Cadence's system design and analysis portfolio and the aforementioned acquisition of BETA CAE.

But, if a deal were to come, it could impact Cadence's deal to acquire Ansys (ANSS), Vruwink said.

"If a large deal is effectuated, we also wonder if this changes investor perceptions around the regulatory proposition facing Synopsys+Ansys; i.e., Altair and suitor would likely have received counsel that clearance is probable, yet investors do not share the same viewpoint on proposed Synopsys+Ansys combination (generally, we find that regulatory approval is seen as the main stumbling block)," Vruwink posited.

data.title: U.S. big tech stocks are driving the nuclear energy momentum – SG
data.description:
Many big U.S. technology stocks (IYW) have announced new investments in nuclear energy and partnerships for power security, as the rise of artificial intelligence is driving secular electricity demand in the U.S.

Almost all hyper-scalers are focused on energy security and demand is driving the nuclear momentum, Manish Kabra, head of U.S. equity strategy at Societe Generale, said in a note.

U.S. electricity demand is expected to exceed supply by next year without more grid investment, said Kabra. “Total U.S. electricity demand is 4K TWh and is growing by 60 basis points per year, and AI will add another 50 basis points to annualized growth, adding the equivalent of three New Yorks to the grid.”

Last year, Microsoft (MSFT) and Google (GOOGL) alone consumed 48 TWh of electricity. That is more power consumed of over 100 countries. Data center load may make up 30-40% of the net new power demand by 2030.

In addition, hyper-scale data centers, such as those used by Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) are responsible for 60-70% of all data center energy use.

Microsoft said it plans to eliminate its carbon emissions by 2030, but they have actually increased by around 40% in the past four years because of data center growth.

Google's (GOOGL) carbon emissions have grown by about 48% in the last five years.

Amazon (AMZN), on the other hand, plans to neutralize all of its data center energy use with renewable power.

According to Bernstein Research analysts, “if AI inference reaches the scale of Google Search, data center electricity demand will see a CAGR of 5% globally and nearly 10% in the U.S.”

These are big U.S. tech companies announcing investments and partnerships focused on small modular reactors:

  • Constellation Energy (CEG) – It has signed a 20-year power purchase agreement with Microsoft (MSFT) to restart the Three Mile Island Nuclear Station Unit 1.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) – Announced a $3.3B AI infrastructure plan in May to grow U.S. data center capacity.
  • Meta Platforms (META) – Also invested $37B in digital infrastructure last February.
  • Google/Alphabet (GOOGL) – The company is working on large-scale data centers that would use more than 1 gigawatts of power. It has also announced $3.3B in investments in two new data centers in South Carolina.
  • Amazon (AMZN) – Last May, it secured a 100% nuclear-powered data center in Pennsylvania to leverage clean energy from the Susquehanna Steam Electric Station, and in March, AWS announced the acquisition of Talen Energy’s data center campus.
  • Oracle (ORCL) – The company said it has secured building permits for a trio of Small Modular Reactors to power a datacenter with over a gigawatt of AI compute capacity.
